You have been asked by the user to prepare/revise a report. 
The user's overall instructions for the dataset are: Analyse the relationship between catchment rainfall and reservoir inflow for all reservoirs (with the applicable data available).
Steps:
1.	Produce a stacked area chart for the total stored water (in ML) including all reservoirs. Include a single line showing the sum of total usable volume in ML for all reservoirs. Include all reservoirs on this chart only, not just those with rainfall and inflow data.
2.	Time series of rainfall (bars) and streamflow (line) – dual y-axis for 2017 only (all reservoirs in one multi-pane chart). 
3.	Lagged rainfall–inflow correlation: Calculate lagged Spearman correlations (0–14 days rainfall leading) and plot correlation (y) vs. Days lag (x). Plot all reservoirs on the same chart.
4.	Antecedent precipitation index calculations (lag=0):
a.	Compute an Antecedent Precipitation Index (API) treating missing rainfall as zero.
b.	Calibrate the API decay factor k over the range 0.85–0.98 (step ≈ 0.01), using Spearman correlation between daily inflow and API at lag = 0 days only.
c.	For each reservoir, identify:
i.	the optimal k that maximises |Spearman ρ|
ii.	the corresponding maximum correlation
iii.	the characteristic catchment memory time
d.	Produce:
i.	one combined plot of Spearman correlation vs k for all reservoirs
ii.	a concise summary table of calibrated k values and memory times, along with an interpretation of what the analysis has shown about the characteristics of each reservoir.
5.	API with lag analysis of 0-14 days. Produce:
a.	Plot of Spearman ρ vs lag at optimal k for all reservoirs on the same chart.
b.	Table of key results from the API lag analysis.
6.	Create a map showing the locations of all reservoirs.
.
The user's specific instructions for preparing/revising the report are: Please write a report based on the analysis provided..


##Information for producing the report

###Source dataset information
File name: MWC_raw_water_reservoirs_2015_2019.xlsx
Data description: Excel workbook containing Melbourne Water/Water Storage-related datasets: a static asset register for major storage dams (identifiers, names, ownership, coordinates, construction date, volumes/levels) and multiple daily time-series tables (2015–2019) for major reservoir catchment rainfall, streamflow into storages, storage volumes/percent full, and bulk water supply delivered.

Sheet name: WS_Storage_Dam_OpenData
Headings: OBJECTID, COMPKEY, COMPTYPE, UNITID, ASSET_NAME, ASSET_ID, USABLE_VOLUME, GAUGE_TWL, EASTING, NORTHING, FIELD_TEAM, ASSET_OWNER, DATE_OF_CONSTRUCTION
Time period: Not applicable to Not applicable
Time step: Not applicable
Time format: Not applicable
Sheet name: MajorCatchmentRainfal
Headings: recorddate, dam, rainfall_mm, ObjectId
Time period: 2015-01-01 to 2019-12-31
Time step: 1 day
Time format: YYYY-MM-DD (stored as datetime64[ns], typically at 00:00:00)
Sheet name: MajorStorageStreamflow
Headings: recorddate, dam, Streamflow into reservoir (ML/d), ObjectId
Time period: 2015-01-01 to 2019-12-31
Time step: 1 day
Time format: YYYY-MM-DD (stored as datetime64[ns], typically at 00:00:00)
Sheet name: StorageVolume
Headings: recorddate, dam, volume (ML), volume_percentfull, ObjectId
Time period: 2015-01-01 to 2019-12-31
Time step: 1 day
Time format: YYYY-MM-DD (stored as datetime64[ns], typically at 00:00:00)
Sheet name: BulkWaterSupply
Headings: recorddate, supplied (ML/d), ObjectId
Time period: 2015-01-01 to 2019-12-31
Time step: 1 day
Time format: YYYY-MM-DD (stored as datetime64[ns], typically at 00:00:00)

###Charts and Tables
These charts and tables have been produced from analysis of the source data:
####Chart: Total Stored Water (All Reservoirs) vs Total Usable Volume
Across 2015–2019, total stored water varies roughly from ~0.85–1.35 million ML, while the summed usable capacity line sits near ~1.9 million ML. This indicates the system operates well below combined usable capacity for most of the period, with a notable drawdown through 2018–2019 before partial recovery late-2019.

Thomson dominates system storage (largest area contribution), so changes in Thomson largely drive the total stored trajectory; Upper Yarra and Cardinia form the next tier, while O’Shannassy/Maroondah and the smaller service storages contribute comparatively minor variability. The widening gap between storage and total usable volume during 2018–2019 suggests increasing supply pressure and/or reduced inflows over that period, consistent with drought-like conditions.

Data source for plotted series: total_stored_vs_usable_volume.csv.
Chart image file name: total_stored_vs_usable_volume.png
Chart image: <img data_store/Qxt3jx/total_stored_vs_usable_volume.png>.

####Chart: 2017 Rainfall (Catchment) and Inflow (Streamflow) Time Series – Multi-Reservoir
All four catchments show a pronounced wet-season response in late winter–spring 2017: rainfall events from ~Aug–Oct coincide with sustained elevated inflows. Peak inflow magnitudes differ strongly by reservoir: Thomson shows the largest peaks (approaching ~3,000 ML/d), Upper Yarra next (~2,500 ML/d), O’Shannassy (~1,600 ML/d) and Maroondah (~1,000 ML/d), reflecting catchment size and runoff efficiency.

In each pane, inflow peaks generally lag rainfall bursts by ~1–3 days and remain elevated for weeks during the wet period, indicating catchment wetting and baseflow contributions beyond “event-only” runoff. The sharp inflow spikes around September and again in late November/December suggest short, intense rainfall events superimposed on differing antecedent moisture conditions.

Data source for plotted series: rainfall_inflow_2017_faceted.csv.
Chart image file name: rainfall_inflow_2017_faceted.png
Chart image: <img data_store/Qxt3jx/rainfall_inflow_2017_faceted.png>.

####Chart: Lagged Rainfall–Inflow Spearman Correlation (0–14 days, Rainfall Leading) – All Reservoirs
Raw daily rainfall shows only weak-to-moderate correlation with inflow across all catchments (ρ ≈ 0.17–0.44), confirming that instantaneous rainfall alone is a poor predictor of streamflow response. Peak correlations occur at lag 0–1 days for most reservoirs, then decay rapidly: by lag 14, correlations drop to ρ ≈ 0.16–0.22.

Maroondah and Thomson show the strongest initial response (ρ₀ ≈ 0.44 and 0.40 respectively), while Upper Yarra is weakest (ρ₀ ≈ 0.27). O'Shannassy uniquely peaks at lag 1 (ρ₁ ≈ 0.40) rather than lag 0, suggesting slightly delayed catchment response or rainfall measurement timing effects.

All curves converge to similar low values (ρ ≈ 0.16–0.22) by lag 14, indicating minimal predictive skill from rainfall events >2 weeks prior. This weak performance motivates the Antecedent Precipitation Index approach, which accounts for cumulative catchment wetness and dramatically improves correlation (ρ ≈ 0.72–0.88 at optimal k, as shown in subsequent API analyses).
Chart image file name: lagged_rainfall_inflow_correlation_redo.png
Chart image: <img data_store/Qxt3jx/lagged_rainfall_inflow_correlation_redo.png>.

####Chart: API Calibration – Spearman Correlation vs k (lag=0, missing rainfall=0)
For all four catchments, Spearman correlation between same-day inflow and API increases monotonically (or near-monotonically) as k rises from 0.85 toward ~0.97–0.98. This indicates inflows are better explained by a rainfall index with substantial persistence (i.e., runoff response depends strongly on antecedent wetness, not just today's rainfall).

Peak correlations within the tested range are high (ρ≈0.72–0.88), substantially higher than raw rainfall–inflow at lag 0, showing API captures catchment "wetting-up" effects:
- Maroondah: best at k≈0.97 (ρ≈0.717), slight drop at 0.98.
- Thomson: best at k≈0.97 (ρ≈0.795), notable drop at 0.98.
- O'Shannassy: best at k≈0.98 (ρ≈0.876).
- Upper Yarra: best at k≈0.98 (ρ≈0.840).

Data source: api_calibration_spearman.csv.
Chart image file name: api_calibration_spearman.png
Chart image: <img data_store/Qxt3jx/api_calibration_spearman.png>.

####Table: API Calibration Summary (Optimal k, Max |ρ|, Memory Time)
Optimal API decay factors are high (k≈0.97–0.98), corresponding to characteristic memory times of ~33–49 days. This implies inflow is strongly controlled by cumulative wetness over the prior month or two, not just short-term rainfall.

O’Shannassy and Upper Yarra both favour k=0.98 (T≈49.5 d) with very strong correlations (ρ≈0.876 and 0.840). Thomson and Maroondah favour k=0.97 (T≈32.8 d) with slightly shorter memory and lower peak correlations (ρ≈0.795 and 0.717).

Interpretation: O’Shannassy/Upper Yarra behave like “slower” catchments (greater subsurface storage/baseflow contribution), while Thomson/Maroondah are somewhat more event-responsive. Note that the optimum for two dams sits at the upper bound of tested k, suggesting even longer memory could potentially increase correlation if k>0.98 were evaluated.

####Chart: API Lag Analysis – Spearman ρ vs Lag (0–14 days) at Optimal k
Using calibrated API, correlations remain very strong even when predicting inflow up to 14 days ahead, with gradual decay rather than the sharp drop seen with raw rainfall.

At lag 0, ρ spans ~0.72 (Maroondah) to ~0.88 (O'Shannassy). By lag 14, correlations are still substantial: Maroondah ~0.52, Thomson ~0.59, Upper Yarra ~0.73, O'Shannassy ~0.76. This persistence reflects the high k values (32–49 day e-folding) and suggests catchment wetness state is a strong predictor of near-term inflow.

Among the four, O'Shannassy and Upper Yarra maintain the highest correlations across all lags, consistent with longer catchment memory and smoother hydrologic response. Maroondah shows the fastest decay, indicating more event-driven variability not fully captured by the simple API model.
Chart image file name: api_lag_analysis.png
Chart image: <img data_store/Qxt3jx/api_lag_analysis.png>.

####Table: API Lag Analysis Key Results (Optimal k)
All dams achieve their maximum |ρ| at lag 0, so API is most informative for same-day inflow; however, forecast skill varies markedly by catchment.

O’Shannassy maintains ρ≥0.75 through the full tested 14-day horizon (ρ14≈0.763), indicating very persistent inflow dynamics once catchment wetness is known. Upper Yarra maintains ρ≥0.75 out to ~10 days (ρ10≈0.754). Thomson only meets ρ≥0.75 out to 1 day (ρ1≈0.751), and Maroondah never reaches 0.75 (ρ0≈0.717), implying a larger share of short-lived/event-driven variability or additional controls (e.g., localised storms not captured by catchment-average rainfall, operational effects).

Converted MGA Zone 55 coordinates place the reservoirs across the greater Melbourne / Victorian Central Highlands region, with a clear eastward spread from metropolitan service storages (e.g., Greenvale, Yan Yean, Sugarloaf, Silvan) to the major water supply catchments in the forested ranges (Upper Yarra, O’Shannassy) and further east to Thomson (largest usable volume). The spatial separation is operationally relevant: storms can be highly localised, so catchment-average rainfall (used in the correlation/API work) will represent different synoptic regimes across the system. The map also highlights why Thomson tends to dominate total system storage while being geographically distinct from the metro storages.

```python
# filename: report.py
"""
Checklist (execution steps):
- Verify required chart image files and CSV summary files are present in the current directory.
- Load summary CSVs (without pandas) and build concise, report-ready tables (≤5 columns).
- Generate a DOCX with cover, updatable TOC/LoF/LoT fields, and consistent header/footer styling.
- Insert figures and tables with automatic numbering captions and reference them in the narrative.
- Save the final document as report.docx in the current directory.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import csv
import os
from datetime import date
from typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional, Tuple

from docx import Document
from docx.enum.table import WD_TABLE_ALIGNMENT, WD_ALIGN_VERTICAL
from docx.enum.text import WD_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT
from docx.oxml import OxmlElement
from docx.oxml.ns import qn
from docx.shared import Mm, Pt, RGBColor
from docx.text.paragraph import Paragraph


# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#                       low-level helpers
# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def configure_page(doc: Document) -> None:
    for s in doc.sections:
        s.page_height = Mm(297)  # A4
        s.page_width = Mm(210)
        s.top_margin = Mm(25)
        s.bottom_margin = Mm(25)
        s.left_margin = Mm(25)
        s.right_margin = Mm(25)


def insert_field(
    paragraph: Paragraph,
    field_code: str,
    font_size: Pt | None = None,
    font_color: RGBColor | None = None,
) -> None:
    run = paragraph.add_run()
    fld = OxmlElement("w:fldSimple")
    fld.set(qn("w:instr"), field_code)
    run._r.append(fld)

    if font_size is not None:
        run.font.size = font_size
    if font_color is not None:
        run.font.color.rgb = font_color


def add_caption(doc: Document, seq_type: str, caption_text: str) -> None:
    seq_type = seq_type.title()
    p = doc.add_paragraph(style="Caption")
    p.add_run(f"{seq_type} ")
    insert_field(p, f"SEQ {seq_type} \\* ARABIC")
    p.add_run(f" – {caption_text}")


def add_header_footer(doc: Document, title: str, report_date: str | None = None) -> None:
    if report_date is None:
        report_date = date.today().strftime("%d %B %Y")

    grey = RGBColor(102, 102, 102)
    fsz10 = Pt(10)

    try:
        footer_style = doc.styles["Footer"]
        footer_style.font.size = fsz10
        footer_style.font.color.rgb = grey
    except KeyError:
        footer_style = None

    for sec in doc.sections:
        # Header
        hp = sec.header.paragraphs[0]
        hp.alignment = WD_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT.CENTER
        hdr_run = hp.add_run(title)
        hdr_run.font.size = fsz10
        hdr_run.font.color.rgb = grey

        # Footer as a 2-col table (date left, page right)
        tbl = sec.footer.add_table(rows=1, cols=2, width=Mm(160))
        tbl.alignment = WD_TABLE_ALIGNMENT.CENTER
        for c in tbl.rows[0].cells:
            c.vertical_alignment = WD_ALIGN_VERTICAL.CENTER

        left_para = tbl.cell(0, 0).paragraphs[0]
        if footer_style is not None:
            left_para.style = footer_style
        d_run = left_para.add_run(report_date)
        d_run.font.size = fsz10
        d_run.font.color.rgb = grey

        right_para = tbl.cell(0, 1).paragraphs[0]
        if footer_style is not None:
            right_para.style = footer_style
        right_para.alignment = WD_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT.RIGHT
        insert_field(right_para, "PAGE  \\* MERGEFORMAT", font_size=fsz10, font_color=grey)


def add_title_page(doc: Document, title: str, report_date: str | None = None) -> None:
    if report_date is None:
        report_date = date.today().strftime("%d %B %Y")

    doc.add_paragraph(title, style="Title").alignment = WD_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT.CENTER
    doc.add_paragraph(report_date, style="Subtitle").alignment = WD_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT.CENTER
    doc.add_page_break()


def add_front_matter(
    doc: Document, include_toc: bool = True, include_lof: bool = True, include_lot: bool = True
) -> None:
    if include_toc:
        doc.add_paragraph("Table of Contents", style="TOC Heading")
        toc = doc.add_paragraph()
        insert_field(toc, 'TOC \\o "1-3" \\h \\z \\u')
        doc.add_paragraph()

    if include_lof:
        doc.add_paragraph("Figures", style="TOC Heading")
        lof = doc.add_paragraph()
        insert_field(lof, 'TOC \\h \\z \\c "Figure"')
        doc.add_paragraph()

    if include_lot:
        doc.add_paragraph("Tables", style="TOC Heading")
        lot = doc.add_paragraph()
        insert_field(lot, 'TOC \\h \\z \\c "Table"')
        doc.add_paragraph()

    if any((include_toc, include_lof, include_lot)):
        doc.add_page_break()


def add_table(
    doc: Document,
    caption: str,
    rows: List[List[str]],
    style: str = "Medium Shading 1 Accent 1",
) -> None:
    if not rows:
        return
    add_caption(doc, "Table", caption)
    t = doc.add_table(rows=len(rows), cols=len(rows[0]))
    t.style = style
    t.alignment = WD_TABLE_ALIGNMENT.CENTER
    t.rows[0].repeat_header = True

    available_width_mm = 160
    num_cols = len(rows[0])
    if num_cols > 0:
        col_width_mm = available_width_mm / num_cols
        for col in t.columns:
            col.width = Mm(col_width_mm)

    for r, row in enumerate(rows):
        for c, txt in enumerate(row):
            t.cell(r, c).text = str(txt)

    doc.add_paragraph()


# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#                     report-specific helpers
# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def file_exists(path: str) -> bool:
    return os.path.isfile(path)


def fmt_int(x: Any) -> str:
    try:
        if x is None or x == "":
            return ""
        return f"{int(round(float(x))):,}"
    except Exception:
        return str(x)


def fmt_float(x: Any, ndp: int = 3) -> str:
    try:
        if x is None or x == "":
            return ""
        return f"{float(x):,.{ndp}f}"
    except Exception:
        return str(x)


def fmt_k(x: Any) -> str:
    try:
        if x is None or x == "":
            return ""
        return f"{float(x):.2f}"
    except Exception:
        return str(x)


def safe_add_picture(doc: Document, img_path: str, width_mm: float = 160) -> bool:
    if not file_exists(img_path):
        p = doc.add_paragraph(
            f"Figure could not be inserted because the image file was not found: {img_path}"
        )
        p.alignment = WD_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT.LEFT
        return False
    doc.add_picture(img_path, width=Mm(width_mm))
    return True


def read_csv_dicts(path: str) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:
    if not file_exists(path):
        return []
    with open(path, "r", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        reader = csv.DictReader(f)
        return [dict(r) for r in reader]


def add_body_paragraph(doc: Document, text: str) -> Paragraph:
    p = doc.add_paragraph(text, style="Body Text")
    p.alignment = WD_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT.JUSTIFY
    return p


def add_spacer_paragraph(doc: Document, lines: int = 1) -> None:
    for _ in range(lines):
        doc.add_paragraph("", style="Body Text")


# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#                          MAIN report
# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def generate_report() -> None:
    doc = Document()
    configure_page(doc)

    report_title = "Catchment Rainfall and Reservoir Inflow Relationship (2015–2019)"
    today_str = date.today().strftime("%d %B %Y")

    # Cover & prelims
    add_title_page(doc, report_title, today_str)
    add_header_footer(doc, report_title, today_str)
    add_front_matter(doc, include_toc=True, include_lof=True, include_lot=True)

    # -----------------------------
    # 1. Introduction
    # -----------------------------
    doc.add_heading("1. Introduction", level=1)
    add_body_paragraph(
        doc,
        "This report evaluates the relationship between catchment rainfall and inflow to major Melbourne Water supply reservoirs "
        "using daily time-series for 2015–2019. The primary focus is quantifying response timing and persistence using lagged rank "
        "correlation and an Antecedent Precipitation Index (API) model, and interpreting implications for short-term inflow predictability "
        "and operational planning.",
    )
    add_spacer_paragraph(doc)

    # -----------------------------
    # 2. Objectives
    # -----------------------------
    doc.add_heading("2. Objectives", level=1)
    add_body_paragraph(
        doc,
        "The objectives of the analysis were to characterise whole-of-system storage behaviour and quantify rainfall–inflow coupling "
        "for reservoirs with applicable rainfall and inflow data, including response lags and effective catchment memory.",
    )
    doc.add_paragraph("The specific objectives were:", style="Body Text")
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "Summarise total stored water across all reservoirs relative to combined usable storage.",
        style="List Bullet",
    )
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "Visualise 2017 rainfall and inflow dynamics to qualitatively assess timing and seasonality.",
        style="List Bullet",
    )
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "Quantify lagged rainfall–inflow Spearman correlation for lags 0–14 days (rainfall leading).",
        style="List Bullet",
    )
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "Calibrate an API decay factor (k) to maximise same-day Spearman correlation between inflow and API.",
        style="List Bullet",
    )
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "Assess API-based predictability over 0–14 day lead times and summarise forecast skill.",
        style="List Bullet",
    )
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "Map reservoir locations to support interpretation of spatial rainfall variability and system operations.",
        style="List Bullet",

    doc.add_heading("4.1 System storage context (all reservoirs)", level=2)

    add_body_paragraph(

    add_spacer_paragraph(doc)

    # 4.6 Spatial context
    doc.add_heading("4.6 Spatial context: reservoir locations", level=2)
    add_body_paragraph(

    safe_add_picture(doc, "reservoir_locations_map.png", width_mm=160)

    # -----------------------------

    doc.add_paragraph(

    add_body_paragraph(
```

```python
# filename: report.py
"""
Checklist (execution steps):
- Verify required chart image files and CSV summary files are present in the current directory.
- Load summary CSVs (without pandas) and build concise, report-ready tables (≤5 columns).
- Generate a DOCX with cover, updatable TOC/LoF/LoT fields, and consistent header/footer styling.
- Insert figures and tables with automatic numbering captions and reference them in the narrative.
- Save the final document as report.docx in the current directory.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import csv
import os
from datetime import date
from typing import List, Dict, Any

from docx import Document
from docx.enum.table import WD_TABLE_ALIGNMENT, WD_ALIGN_VERTICAL
from docx.enum.text import WD_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT
from docx.oxml import OxmlElement
from docx.oxml.ns import qn
from docx.shared import Mm, Pt, RGBColor
from docx.text.paragraph import Paragraph


# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#                       low-level helpers
# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def configure_page(doc: Document) -> None:
    for s in doc.sections:
        s.page_height = Mm(297)  # A4
        s.page_width = Mm(210)
        s.top_margin = Mm(25)
        s.bottom_margin = Mm(25)
        s.left_margin = Mm(25)
        s.right_margin = Mm(25)


def insert_field(
    paragraph: Paragraph,
    field_code: str,
    font_size: Pt | None = None,
    font_color: RGBColor | None = None,
) -> None:
    run = paragraph.add_run()
    fld = OxmlElement("w:fldSimple")
    fld.set(qn("w:instr"), field_code)
    run._r.append(fld)

    if font_size is not None:
        run.font.size = font_size
    if font_color is not None:
        run.font.color.rgb = font_color


def add_caption(doc: Document, seq_type: str, caption_text: str) -> Paragraph:
    seq_type = seq_type.title()
    p = doc.add_paragraph(style="Caption")
    p.add_run(f"{seq_type} ")
    insert_field(p, f"SEQ {seq_type} \\* ARABIC")
    p.add_run(f" – {caption_text}")
    # Keep caption with the next block (figure/table)
    p.paragraph_format.keep_with_next = True
    return p


def add_header_footer(doc: Document, title: str, report_date: str | None = None) -> None:
    if report_date is None:
        report_date = date.today().strftime("%d %B %Y")

    grey = RGBColor(102, 102, 102)
    fsz10 = Pt(10)

    try:
        footer_style = doc.styles["Footer"]
        footer_style.font.size = fsz10
        footer_style.font.color.rgb = grey
    except KeyError:
        footer_style = None

    for sec in doc.sections:
        # Header
        hp = sec.header.paragraphs[0]
        hp.alignment = WD_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT.CENTER
        hdr_run = hp.add_run(title)
        hdr_run.font.size = fsz10
        hdr_run.font.color.rgb = grey

        # Footer as a 2-col table (date left, page right)
        tbl = sec.footer.add_table(rows=1, cols=2, width=Mm(160))
        tbl.alignment = WD_TABLE_ALIGNMENT.CENTER
        for c in tbl.rows[0].cells:
            c.vertical_alignment = WD_ALIGN_VERTICAL.CENTER

        left_para = tbl.cell(0, 0).paragraphs[0]
        if footer_style is not None:
            left_para.style = footer_style
        d_run = left_para.add_run(report_date)
        d_run.font.size = fsz10
        d_run.font.color.rgb = grey

        right_para = tbl.cell(0, 1).paragraphs[0]
        if footer_style is not None:
            right_para.style = footer_style
        right_para.alignment = WD_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT.RIGHT
        insert_field(right_para, "PAGE  \\* MERGEFORMAT", font_size=fsz10, font_color=grey)


def add_title_page(doc: Document, title: str, report_date: str | None = None) -> None:
    if report_date is None:
        report_date = date.today().strftime("%d %B %Y")

    doc.add_paragraph(title, style="Title").alignment = WD_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT.CENTER
    doc.add_paragraph(report_date, style="Subtitle").alignment = WD_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT.CENTER
    doc.add_page_break()


def add_front_matter(
    doc: Document, include_toc: bool = True, include_lof: bool = True, include_lot: bool = True
) -> None:
    if include_toc:
        doc.add_paragraph("Table of Contents", style="TOC Heading")
        toc = doc.add_paragraph()
        insert_field(toc, 'TOC \\o "1-3" \\h \\z \\u')
        doc.add_paragraph()

    if include_lof:
        doc.add_paragraph("Figures", style="TOC Heading")
        lof = doc.add_paragraph()
        insert_field(lof, 'TOC \\h \\z \\c "Figure"')
        doc.add_paragraph()

    if include_lot:
        doc.add_paragraph("Tables", style="TOC Heading")
        lot = doc.add_paragraph()
        insert_field(lot, 'TOC \\h \\z \\c "Table"')
        doc.add_paragraph()

    if any((include_toc, include_lof, include_lot)):
        doc.add_page_break()


def add_table(
    doc: Document,
    caption: str,
    rows: List[List[str]],
    style: str = "Medium Shading 1 Accent 1",
) -> None:
    if not rows:
        return

    # Caption (kept with next by add_caption)
    add_caption(doc, "Table", caption)

    t = doc.add_table(rows=len(rows), cols=len(rows[0]))
    t.style = style
    t.alignment = WD_TABLE_ALIGNMENT.CENTER
    t.rows[0].repeat_header = True

    available_width_mm = 160
    num_cols = len(rows[0])
    if num_cols > 0:
        col_width_mm = available_width_mm / num_cols
        for col in t.columns:
            col.width = Mm(col_width_mm)

    for r, row in enumerate(rows):
        for c, txt in enumerate(row):
            t.cell(r, c).text = str(txt)

    doc.add_paragraph()


# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#                     report-specific helpers
# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def file_exists(path: str) -> bool:
    return os.path.isfile(path)


def fmt_int(x: Any) -> str:
    try:
        if x is None or x == "":
            return ""
        return f"{int(round(float(x))):,}"
    except Exception:
        return str(x)


def fmt_float(x: Any, ndp: int = 3) -> str:
    try:
        if x is None or x == "":
            return ""
        return f"{float(x):,.{ndp}f}"
    except Exception:
        return str(x)


def fmt_k(x: Any) -> str:
    try:
        if x is None or x == "":
            return ""
        return f"{float(x):.2f}"
    except Exception:
        return str(x)


def read_csv_dicts(path: str) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:
    if not file_exists(path):
        return []
    with open(path, "r", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        reader = csv.DictReader(f)
        return [dict(r) for r in reader]


def add_body_paragraph(doc: Document, text: str, keep_with_next: bool = False) -> Paragraph:
    p = doc.add_paragraph(text, style="Body Text")
    p.alignment = WD_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT.JUSTIFY
    if keep_with_next:
        p.paragraph_format.keep_with_next = True
    return p


def add_spacer_paragraph(doc: Document, lines: int = 1) -> None:
    for _ in range(lines):
        doc.add_paragraph("", style="Body Text")


def add_heading(doc: Document, text: str, level: int = 1) -> Paragraph:
    p = doc.add_heading(text, level=level)
    # Keep headings with the first paragraph under them to avoid orphan headings
    p.paragraph_format.keep_with_next = True
    return p


def safe_add_picture(doc: Document, img_path: str, width_mm: float = 160) -> bool:
    if not file_exists(img_path):
        p = doc.add_paragraph(
            f"Figure could not be inserted because the image file was not found: {img_path}",
            style="Body Text",
        )
        p.alignment = WD_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT.LEFT
        return False
    pic_par = doc.add_paragraph()
    run = pic_par.add_run()
    run.add_picture(img_path, width=Mm(width_mm))
    # Keep picture with the caption that follows
    pic_par.paragraph_format.keep_with_next = True
    return True


# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#                          MAIN report
# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def generate_report() -> None:
    doc = Document()
    configure_page(doc)

    report_title = "Catchment Rainfall and Reservoir Inflow Relationship (2015–2019)"
    today_str = date.today().strftime("%d %B %Y")

    # Cover & prelims
    add_title_page(doc, report_title, today_str)
    add_header_footer(doc, report_title, today_str)
    add_front_matter(doc, include_toc=True, include_lof=True, include_lot=True)

    # -----------------------------
    # 1. Introduction
    # -----------------------------
    add_heading(doc, "1. Introduction", level=1)
    add_body_paragraph(
        doc,
        "This report evaluates the relationship between catchment rainfall and inflow to major Melbourne Water supply reservoirs "
        "using daily time-series for 2015–2019. The primary focus is quantifying response timing and persistence using lagged rank "
        "correlation and an Antecedent Precipitation Index (API) model, and interpreting implications for short-term inflow predictability "
        "and operational planning.",
    )
    add_spacer_paragraph(doc)

    # -----------------------------
    # 2. Objectives
    # -----------------------------
    add_heading(doc, "2. Objectives", level=1)
    add_body_paragraph(
        doc,
        "The objectives of the analysis were to characterise whole-of-system storage behaviour and quantify rainfall–inflow coupling "
        "for reservoirs with applicable rainfall and inflow data, including response lags and effective catchment memory.",
    )
    doc.add_paragraph("The specific objectives were:", style="Body Text")
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "Summarise total stored water across all reservoirs relative to combined usable storage.",
        style="List Bullet",
    )
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "Visualise 2017 rainfall and inflow dynamics to qualitatively assess timing and seasonality.",
        style="List Bullet",
    )
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "Quantify lagged rainfall–inflow Spearman correlation for lags 0–14 days (rainfall leading).",
        style="List Bullet",
    )
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "Calibrate an API decay factor (k) to maximise same-day Spearman correlation between inflow and API.",
        style="List Bullet",
    )
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "Assess API-based predictability over 0–14 day lead times and summarise forecast skill.",
        style="List Bullet",
    )
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "Map reservoir locations to support interpretation of spatial rainfall variability and system operations.",
        style="List Bullet",
    )
    add_spacer_paragraph(doc)

    # -----------------------------
    # 3. Description of Dataset
    # -----------------------------
    add_heading(doc, "3. Description of Dataset", level=1)
    add_body_paragraph(
        doc,
        "The source dataset is an Excel workbook containing (i) a static asset register for major reservoirs "
        "(including usable volume and coordinates) and (ii) daily time-series (2015–2019) for catchment rainfall, "
        "streamflow into reservoirs (inflow), and storage volume/percent full. Rainfall and inflow time series were available for "
        "Upper Yarra, Thomson, O’Shannassy, and Maroondah. Storage volumes were available for a broader set of service and supply storages "
        "and were used to compute total system storage.",
    )
    add_spacer_paragraph(doc)

    # -----------------------------
    # 4. Analysis
    # -----------------------------
    add_heading(doc, "4. Analysis", level=1)

    # 4.1 Total storage vs usable capacity
    add_heading(doc, "4.1 System storage context (all reservoirs)", level=2)
    intro = add_body_paragraph(
        doc,
        "Figure 1 summarises total stored water across all reservoirs as a stacked area chart, alongside the summed usable volume. "
        "This provides context for the rainfall–inflow relationships by indicating periods of drawdown and refill at a system level.",
        keep_with_next=True,  # keep with the figure that follows
    )
    safe_add_picture(doc, "total_stored_vs_usable_volume.png", width_mm=160)
    add_caption(doc, "Figure", "Total stored water (all reservoirs) versus total usable volume.")

    # -----------------------------
    # 5. Key Findings

    add_heading(doc, "5. Key Findings", level=1)
    add_body_paragraph(
```

```python
# filename: report.py
"""
Checklist (execution steps):
- Verify required chart image files and CSV summary files are present in the current directory.
- Load summary CSVs (without pandas) and build concise, report-ready tables (≤5 columns).
- Generate a DOCX with cover, updatable TOC/LoF/LoT fields, and consistent header/footer styling.
- Insert figures and tables with automatic numbering captions and reference them in the narrative.
- Save the final document as report.docx in the current directory.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import csv
import os
from datetime import date
from typing import List, Dict, Any

from docx import Document
from docx.enum.table import WD_TABLE_ALIGNMENT, WD_ALIGN_VERTICAL
from docx.enum.text import WD_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT
from docx.oxml import OxmlElement
from docx.oxml.ns import qn
from docx.shared import Mm, Pt, RGBColor
from docx.text.paragraph import Paragraph


# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#                       low-level helpers
# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def configure_page(doc: Document) -> None:
    for s in doc.sections:
        s.page_height = Mm(297)  # A4
        s.page_width = Mm(210)
        s.top_margin = Mm(25)
        s.bottom_margin = Mm(25)
        s.left_margin = Mm(25)
        s.right_margin = Mm(25)


def insert_field(
    paragraph: Paragraph,
    field_code: str,
    font_size: Pt | None = None,
    font_color: RGBColor | None = None,
) -> None:
    run = paragraph.add_run()
    fld = OxmlElement("w:fldSimple")
    fld.set(qn("w:instr"), field_code)
    run._r.append(fld)

    if font_size is not None:
        run.font.size = font_size
    if font_color is not None:
        run.font.color.rgb = font_color


def add_caption(doc: Document, seq_type: str, caption_text: str) -> Paragraph:
    seq_type = seq_type.title()
    p = doc.add_paragraph(style="Caption")
    p.add_run(f"{seq_type} ")
    insert_field(p, f"SEQ {seq_type} \\* ARABIC")
    p.add_run(f" – {caption_text}")
    # Keep caption with the next block (table/picture)
    p.paragraph_format.keep_with_next = True
    return p


def add_header_footer(doc: Document, title: str, report_date: str | None = None) -> None:
    if report_date is None:
        report_date = date.today().strftime("%d %B %Y")

    grey = RGBColor(102, 102, 102)
    fsz10 = Pt(10)

    try:
        footer_style = doc.styles["Footer"]
        footer_style.font.size = fsz10
        footer_style.font.color.rgb = grey
    except KeyError:
        footer_style = None

    for sec in doc.sections:
        # Header
        hp = sec.header.paragraphs[0]
        hp.alignment = WD_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT.CENTER
        hdr_run = hp.add_run(title)
        hdr_run.font.size = fsz10
        hdr_run.font.color.rgb = grey

        # Footer as a 2-col table (date left, page right)
        tbl = sec.footer.add_table(rows=1, cols=2, width=Mm(160))
        tbl.alignment = WD_TABLE_ALIGNMENT.CENTER
        for c in tbl.rows[0].cells:
            c.vertical_alignment = WD_ALIGN_VERTICAL.CENTER

        left_para = tbl.cell(0, 0).paragraphs[0]
        if footer_style is not None:
            left_para.style = footer_style
        d_run = left_para.add_run(report_date)
        d_run.font.size = fsz10
        d_run.font.color.rgb = grey

        right_para = tbl.cell(0, 1).paragraphs[0]
        if footer_style is not None:
            right_para.style = footer_style
        right_para.alignment = WD_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT.RIGHT
        insert_field(right_para, "PAGE  \\* MERGEFORMAT", font_size=fsz10, font_color=grey)


def add_title_page(doc: Document, title: str, report_date: str | None = None) -> None:
    if report_date is None:
        report_date = date.today().strftime("%d %B %Y")

    doc.add_paragraph(title, style="Title").alignment = WD_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT.CENTER
    doc.add_paragraph(report_date, style="Subtitle").alignment = WD_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT.CENTER
    doc.add_page_break()


def add_front_matter(
    doc: Document, include_toc: bool = True, include_lof: bool = True, include_lot: bool = True
) -> None:
    if include_toc:
        doc.add_paragraph("Table of Contents", style="TOC Heading")
        toc = doc.add_paragraph()
        insert_field(toc, 'TOC \\o "1-3" \\h \\z \\u')
        doc.add_paragraph()

    if include_lof:
        doc.add_paragraph("Figures", style="TOC Heading")
        lof = doc.add_paragraph()
        insert_field(lof, 'TOC \\h \\z \\c "Figure"')
        doc.add_paragraph()

    if include_lot:
        doc.add_paragraph("Tables", style="TOC Heading")
        lot = doc.add_paragraph()
        insert_field(lot, 'TOC \\h \\z \\c "Table"')
        doc.add_paragraph()

    if any((include_toc, include_lof, include_lot)):
        doc.add_page_break()


def _set_row_cant_split(row) -> None:
    """
    Prevent a Word table row from splitting across pages.
    """
    tr = row._tr
    trPr = tr.get_or_add_trPr()
    cant = trPr.find(qn("w:cantSplit"))
    if cant is None:
        cant = OxmlElement("w:cantSplit")
        trPr.append(cant)


def add_table(
    doc: Document,
    caption: str,
    rows: List[List[str]],
    style: str = "Medium Shading 1 Accent 1",
) -> None:
    if not rows:
        return

    add_caption(doc, "Table", caption)

    t = doc.add_table(rows=len(rows), cols=len(rows[0]))
    t.style = style
    t.alignment = WD_TABLE_ALIGNMENT.CENTER

    # Repeat header row on each page (if table does break)
    t.rows[0].repeat_header = True

    # Prevent row splitting; improves readability when page breaks occur near a table
    for rr in t.rows:
        _set_row_cant_split(rr)

    # Fit to page width by distributing columns
    available_width_mm = 160
    num_cols = len(rows[0])
    if num_cols > 0:
        col_width_mm = available_width_mm / num_cols
        for col in t.columns:
            col.width = Mm(col_width_mm)

    for r, row in enumerate(rows):
        for c, txt in enumerate(row):
            t.cell(r, c).text = str(txt)

    doc.add_paragraph()


# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#                     report-specific helpers
# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def file_exists(path: str) -> bool:
    return os.path.isfile(path)


def fmt_int(x: Any) -> str:
    try:
        if x is None or x == "":
            return ""
        return f"{int(round(float(x))):,}"
    except Exception:
        return str(x)


def fmt_float(x: Any, ndp: int = 3) -> str:
    try:
        if x is None or x == "":
            return ""
        return f"{float(x):,.{ndp}f}"
    except Exception:
        return str(x)


def fmt_k(x: Any) -> str:
    try:
        if x is None or x == "":
            return ""
        return f"{float(x):.2f}"
    except Exception:
        return str(x)


def read_csv_dicts(path: str) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:
    if not file_exists(path):
        return []
    with open(path, "r", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        reader = csv.DictReader(f)
        return [dict(r) for r in reader]


def add_body_paragraph(doc: Document, text: str, keep_with_next: bool = False) -> Paragraph:
    p = doc.add_paragraph(text, style="Body Text")
    p.alignment = WD_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT.JUSTIFY
    if keep_with_next:
        p.paragraph_format.keep_with_next = True
    return p


def add_spacer_paragraph(doc: Document, lines: int = 1) -> None:
    for _ in range(lines):
        doc.add_paragraph("", style="Body Text")


def add_heading(doc: Document, text: str, level: int = 1) -> Paragraph:
    p = doc.add_heading(text, level=level)
    p.paragraph_format.keep_with_next = True
    return p


def safe_add_picture(doc: Document, img_path: str, width_mm: float = 160) -> bool:
    if not file_exists(img_path):
        p = doc.add_paragraph(
            f"Figure could not be inserted because the image file was not found: {img_path}",
            style="Body Text",
        )
        p.alignment = WD_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT.LEFT
        return False

    pic_par = doc.add_paragraph()
    run = pic_par.add_run()
    run.add_picture(img_path, width=Mm(width_mm))
    pic_par.paragraph_format.keep_with_next = True
    return True


# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#                          MAIN report
# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def generate_report() -> None:
    doc = Document()
    configure_page(doc)

    report_title = "Catchment Rainfall and Reservoir Inflow Relationship (2015–2019)"
    today_str = date.today().strftime("%d %B %Y")

    # Cover & prelims
    add_title_page(doc, report_title, today_str)
    add_header_footer(doc, report_title, today_str)
    add_front_matter(doc, include_toc=True, include_lof=True, include_lot=True)

    # 1. Introduction
    add_heading(doc, "1. Introduction", level=1)
    add_body_paragraph(
        doc,
        "This report evaluates the relationship between catchment rainfall and inflow to major Melbourne Water supply reservoirs "
        "using daily time-series for 2015–2019. The primary focus is quantifying response timing and persistence using lagged rank "
        "correlation and an Antecedent Precipitation Index (API) model, and interpreting implications for short-term inflow predictability "
        "and operational planning.",
    )
    add_spacer_paragraph(doc)

    # 2. Objectives
    add_heading(doc, "2. Objectives", level=1)
    add_body_paragraph(
        doc,
        "The objectives of the analysis were to characterise whole-of-system storage behaviour and quantify rainfall–inflow coupling "
        "for reservoirs with applicable rainfall and inflow data, including response lags and effective catchment memory.",
    )
    doc.add_paragraph("The specific objectives were:", style="Body Text")
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "Summarise total stored water across all reservoirs relative to combined usable storage.", style="List Bullet"
    )
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "Visualise 2017 rainfall and inflow dynamics to qualitatively assess timing and seasonality.", style="List Bullet"
    )
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "Quantify lagged rainfall–inflow Spearman correlation for lags 0–14 days (rainfall leading).", style="List Bullet"
    )
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "Calibrate an API decay factor (k) to maximise same-day Spearman correlation between inflow and API.", style="List Bullet"
    )
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "Assess API-based predictability over 0–14 day lead times and summarise forecast skill.", style="List Bullet"
    )
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "Map reservoir locations to support interpretation of spatial rainfall variability and system operations.", style="List Bullet"
    )
    add_spacer_paragraph(doc)

    # 3. Description of Dataset
    add_heading(doc, "3. Description of Dataset", level=1)
    add_body_paragraph(
        doc,
        "The source dataset is an Excel workbook containing (i) a static asset register for major reservoirs "
        "(including usable volume and coordinates) and (ii) daily time-series (2015–2019) for catchment rainfall, "
        "streamflow into reservoirs (inflow), and storage volume/percent full. Rainfall and inflow time series were available for "
        "Upper Yarra, Thomson, O’Shannassy, and Maroondah. Storage volumes were available for a broader set of service and supply storages "
        "and were used to compute total system storage.",
    )
    add_spacer_paragraph(doc)

    # 4. Analysis
    add_heading(doc, "4. Analysis", level=1)

    # 4.1
    add_heading(doc, "4.1 System storage context (all reservoirs)", level=2)
    add_body_paragraph(
        doc,
        "Figure 1 summarises total stored water across all reservoirs as a stacked area chart, alongside the summed usable volume. "
        "This provides context for the rainfall–inflow relationships by indicating periods of drawdown and refill at a system level.",
        keep_with_next=True,
    )
    safe_add_picture(doc, "total_stored_vs_usable_volume.png", width_mm=160)
    add_caption(doc, "Figure", "Total stored water (all reservoirs) versus total usable volume.")
    add_spacer_paragraph(doc)

    add_body_paragraph(
        doc,
        "Across 2015–2019, total stored water generally remained below combined usable capacity, with a notable drawdown through 2018–2019 "
        "before partial recovery late-2019. Storage variability is dominated by Thomson (largest usable volume), with Upper Yarra and Cardinia "
        "as secondary contributors. The widening storage-to-capacity gap during 2018–2019 is consistent with reduced catchment yield and/or "
        "increased net offtakes during a dry period.",
    )
    add_spacer_paragraph(doc)

    # 4.2
    add_heading(doc, "4.2 Rainfall and inflow time-series behaviour (2017)", level=2)
    add_body_paragraph(
        doc,

    safe_add_picture(doc, "rainfall_inflow_2017_faceted.png", width_mm=160)
    add_caption(doc, "Figure", "2017 rainfall (catchment) and inflow (streamflow) time series by reservoir (dual y-axis).")
    add_spacer_paragraph(doc)

    add_body_paragraph(
        doc,
        "All catchments show a pronounced wet-season response in late winter to spring (approximately August to October), where "
        "frequent rainfall events coincide with sustained elevated inflows. Inflow peaks generally lag rainfall bursts by about 1–3 days, "
        "with elevated inflow persisting for weeks during the wet period. This sustained response indicates that baseflow and stored catchment "
        "moisture are important controls, rather than a purely event-driven runoff mechanism.",
    )
    add_spacer_paragraph(doc)

    # 4.3
    add_heading(doc, "4.3 Lagged rainfall–inflow correlation (0–14 days)", level=2)
    add_body_paragraph(

        "Raw daily rainfall exhibits only weak-to-moderate correlation with inflow, with peak correlations typically occurring at lag 0–1 days "

    add_spacer_paragraph(doc)

    # 4.4
    add_heading(doc, "4.4 API calibration (lag = 0 days, missing rainfall treated as 0)", level=2)
    add_body_paragraph(

        "An Antecedent Precipitation Index (API) was computed for each catchment using a simple exponential decay formulation, treating missing "

        "between same-day inflow and API.",
        keep_with_next=True,
    )
    safe_add_picture(doc, "api_calibration_spearman.png", width_mm=160)
    add_caption(doc, "Figure", "API calibration: Spearman correlation versus API decay factor k (lag = 0 days).")
    add_spacer_paragraph(doc)

    add_body_paragraph(
        doc,
        "Figure 4 shows that correlation generally increases as k approaches 0.97–0.98, indicating that inflow is better explained by an index with "

    add_spacer_paragraph(doc)

    calib_rows = read_csv_dicts("api_calibration_summary.csv")
    add_body_paragraph(

    for r in calib_rows:

                r.get("dam", ""),

    add_table(doc, "API calibration summary (optimal k, same-day correlation, and memory time).", t1)

    add_body_paragraph(
        doc,
        "The calibrated k values are high (approximately 0.97–0.98), corresponding to characteristic memory times on the order of one to two months. "

    add_heading(doc, "4.5 API lead-time analysis (0–14 days) using optimal k", level=2)

    add_caption(doc, "Figure", "Reservoir locations derived from the asset register (MGA Zone 55 converted to latitude/longitude).")

    # 5. Key Findings
    add_heading(doc, "5. Key Findings", level=1)
    add_body_paragraph(
        doc,
        "The key findings below synthesise results across Figures 1–6 and Tables 1–2, linking system storage behaviour with catchment response "
        "timing and persistence.",
    )
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "System storage remained below combined usable capacity throughout 2015–2019, with a material drawdown during 2018–2019 largely driven by Thomson (Figure 1).",
        style="List Bullet",
    )
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "2017 time series show a wet-season regime with sustained inflow elevation beyond individual rainfall events, consistent with catchment wetting-up and baseflow contributions (Figure 2).",
        style="List Bullet",
    )
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "Raw rainfall is a weak predictor of inflow at daily resolution; peak Spearman correlation is modest and largely confined to lag 0–1 days (Figure 3).",

        "API calibration yields high optimal decay factors (k ≈ 0.97–0.98) and long catchment memory times (~33–49 days), indicating strong dependence on antecedent wetness (Figure 4, Table 1).",
        style="List Bullet",
    )
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "API substantially improves correlation and retains predictive skill to 14 days, particularly for O’Shannassy and Upper Yarra, which exhibit the most persistent behaviour (Figure 5, Table 2).",

    )
    doc.add_paragraph(
        "Reservoirs are geographically dispersed, supporting the interpretation that spatial rainfall variability and differing catchment characteristics contribute to differences in correlation and persistence (Figure 6).",

    )
    add_spacer_paragraph(doc)

    # 6. Recommendations
    add_heading(doc, "6. Recommendations", level=1)
    add_body_paragraph(
        doc,
        "The analyses indicate that a persistent wetness state explains inflow substantially better than raw daily rainfall. The following recommendations "

        "Extend API calibration beyond k = 0.98 (e.g., up to 0.995) for catchments that peaked at the current upper bound (Table 1), to confirm whether longer memory further improves performance.",

    doc.add_paragraph(

    )
    doc.add_paragraph(

        "Develop a simple operational forecasting tool using calibrated API with lead-time performance benchmarks (Table 2), complemented by rainfall forecast scenarios for event-driven peaks.",
```
